descriptions.Major: Bright, stable, and familiar. The default language for pop, folk, classical, and most functional harmony.
descriptions.Dorian: Minor with a raised 6th. Very usable for modal jazz, funk, and grooving minor vamp writing.
descriptions.Phrygian: Dark, tense, and rooty. Common in metal, flamenco-flavored writing, and dramatic minor textures.
descriptions.Lydian: Bright and airy because of the raised 4th. Often used for film, fusion, and floating major sounds.
descriptions.Mixolydian: Major with a flat 7. Common in rock, blues-rock, country, and dominant-vamp improvising.
descriptions.Natural Minor: Darker and more reflective than major. Common in rock, folk, film, and minor-key harmony.
descriptions.Locrian: Tense and unstable because of the flat 5. Rare as a tonic scale, but useful for half-diminished colors.
descriptions.Harmonic Minor: Minor with a raised 7th. Strong classical and neoclassical gravity, and a common jazz source for dominant and exotic colors.
descriptions.Melodic Minor: Minor with raised 6th and 7th ascending. A major source for modern jazz harmony, altered colors, and modal extensions.
descriptions.Major Pentatonic: Open, consonant, and friendly. One of the most widely used melodic collections in global folk and popular music.
descriptions.Minor Pentatonic: The standard blues and rock sound. Easy to hear, easy to improvise with, and durable across genres.
descriptions.Double Harmonic Major: Also called Byzantine. Strongly color-laden, with an unmistakably Eastern or medieval flavor.
descriptions.Minor Blues: Minor pentatonic with an added flat 5 color. The classic blues vocabulary in a compact shape.
descriptions.Major Blues: Major pentatonic with a blues inflection. Bright but slightly gritty; common in blues, rock, and country phrasing.
descriptions.Locrian #6: Locrian with a natural 6. A less unstable Locrian color; useful for m7b5-related harmony and modal jazz.
descriptions.Ionian #5: Major with a raised 5th. Sounds bright but slightly uncanny; useful for augmented-major colors.
descriptions.Dorian #4: Dorian with a raised 4th. A modern minor-modal color that sits well over static vamps and fusion harmony.
descriptions.Phrygian Dominant: Phrygian with a major 3rd. A strong dominant/exotic sound, common in flamenco, metal, and world-music color.
descriptions.Lydian #2: Lydian with a raised 2nd. Very bright and unusual; useful for modern melodic lines and synthetic harmony.
descriptions.Ultra-Locrian: Highly altered and unstable. Strongly tension-filled, often treated as a diminished or altered dominant color.
descriptions.Dorian b2: Minor with a flat 2. Dark, slightly Phrygian, and common in modern jazz modal writing.
descriptions.Lydian Augmented: Lydian with a raised 5th. Floating and glossy; often associated with modern film and fusion harmony.
descriptions.Lydian Dominant: Lydian with a flat 7. Bright dominant color, very common in jazz harmony over V chords and static dominants.
descriptions.Mixolydian b6: Dominant with a flat 6. A darker dominant sound, useful for minor-key dominant motion.
descriptions.Locrian #2: Half-diminished color with a natural 2. Very common in jazz for m7b5 and minor ii chords.
descriptions.Altered Scale: Fully altered dominant color. One of the main modern-jazz options for altered V harmony.
descriptions.Whole Tone: Floating, ambiguous, and symmetric. Famous in impressionism, film cue writing, and coloristic harmony.
descriptions.Whole-Half Diminished Scale: Octatonic diminished collection starting with whole step. Strong in jazz, classical, and dominant-diminished harmony.
descriptions.Chromatic: All 12 notes. Useful for approach tones, voice leading, and dense modern writing.
descriptions.Hungarian Minor: Minor with a raised 4th and the characteristic augmented-second sound. Used in folk-derived and dramatic writing.
descriptions.Neapolitan Major: Major with a flat 2 and flat 6 color. Classical and film-friendly, with a lush and tense first-degree pull.
descriptions.Neapolitan Minor: Minor with a Neapolitan inflection. Darker and more chromatic than the major version.
descriptions.Suspended Pentatonic: A suspended, open sound that avoids a firm third. Useful for modal riffs and neutral-sounding melodies.
descriptions.Blues Minor: Close to the minor blues sound, with a strong bluesy minor gravity and a little extra color.
descriptions.Blues Major: Major-pentatonic based but with a blues shape. Bright, playful, and good for major-key blues phrasing.
descriptions.Harmonic Major: Major with a flat 6. A less common but very useful source for jazz and color harmony.
descriptions.Enigmatic: Highly unusual and chromatic. Historically famous as a puzzle scale and useful for eerie, unstable color.
descriptions.Persian: Exotic and asymmetric, with a strong middle-eastern or cinematic color.
descriptions.Half-Whole Diminished Scale: Octatonic diminished collection starting with half step. Very common over dominant chords and altered dominant language.
descriptions.Augmented Scale: Symmetric hexatonic collection based around augmented-triad symmetry. Useful for modern harmony and altered-color lines.
descriptions.Messiaen Mode 3: Also known as the Tcherepnin scale. A dense nine-note synthetic collection with strong chromatic color.
descriptions.Messiaen Mode 4: A symmetrical limited-transposition mode with a dense modern-classical sound.
descriptions.Messiaen Mode 5: A compact synthetic collection with a very specific modern color.
descriptions.Messiaen Mode 6: Another limited-transposition collection used for coloristic, symmetrical harmony.
descriptions.Messiaen Mode 7: The most extended of the named modes here, with a rich and very dense synthetic color.
descriptions.Major Bebop: A major scale with an added passing tone between the 5th and 6th, commonly used for straight eighth-note bebop lines.
descriptions.Dominant Bebop: A Mixolydian-flavored bebop scale with an added major 7 passing tone for dominant lines.
descriptions.Minor Bebop: A minor bebop collection, commonly treated as a Dorian-type scale with an added major 7 passing tone.
descriptions.Major Hexatonic: A six-note major-flavored collection that keeps a bright, open sound with less density than a full heptatonic scale.
descriptions.Minor Hexatonic: A six-note minor-flavored collection that sits between pentatonic simplicity and full minor-scale color.
descriptions.Prometheus: A synthetic six-note scale associated with Scriabin, with a bright raised-4 dominant color.
